title: astragalus sesamoides
intro: Astragalus sesamoides, also known as the woolly milk vetch or woolly vetch, is a perennial plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its adaptogenic and immune-boosting properties, which are attributed to its high content of polysaccharides and saponins. The medicinal properties of Astragalus sesamoides include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects, which can help to protect against stress, fatigue, and certain diseases. This plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, such as teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is often used to support immune function, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve overall well-being.
